title: Approximating Hyperbolic Lattices by Cubulations
speaker: Eduardo Reyes
abstract:
The fundamental group of an n-dimensional closed hyperbolic manifold admits a natural isometric action on the hyperbolic space Hn. If n is at most 3 or the manifold is arithmetic of simplest type, then the group also admits many geometric actions on CAT(0)
 cube complexes. I will talk about a joint work with Nic Brody in which we approximate the asymptotic geometry of the action on Hn
 by actions on these complexes, solving a conjecture of Futer and Wise. The main tool is a codimension-1 generalization of the space of geodesic currents introduced by Bonahon.
tags: Joint IAS/PU Groups and Dynamics Seminar

proof_stages.label: Comparison problem for hyperbolic-lattice actions
proof_stages.summary: The talk fixes fundamental groups of closed hyperbolic manifolds and sets up the problem of comparing their natural actions on hyperbolic space with their geometric actions on CAT(0) cube complexes.

proof_stages.label: Marked length functions as comparison invariants
proof_stages.summary: For any isometric group action, the stable translation length is defined and motivated by marked length spectrum rigidity results, making it the invariant used to compare different actions.

proof_stages.label: Cubulations of the relevant manifold groups
proof_stages.summary: CAT(0) cube complexes are introduced via their local nonpositive-curvature and simple-connectivity conditions, and known cubulation theorems are recalled for surface groups, closed hyperbolic 3-manifold groups, and arithmetic simple-type cases.

proof_stages.label: Cubical marked-length rigidity
proof_stages.summary: A theorem for hyperbolic groups acting geometrically on CAT(0) cube complexes says that, under appropriate irreducibility or essentiality hypotheses, the combinatorial marked length spectrum determines the cubical action up to equivariant isometry.

proof_stages.label: Main approximation theorem
proof_stages.summary: The central result asserts that for the specified hyperbolic lattice there is a sequence of cubulations whose combinatorial length functions approximate the hyperbolic length function up to multiplicative constants with ratio tending to one.

proof_stages.label: Transfer to random quotient estimates
proof_stages.summary: The approximation theorem is explained as a way to transfer Futer--Wise-style cubical random-quotient tools to random quotients measured using the hyperbolic action.

proof_stages.label: Measure-theoretic proof strategy in dimension three
proof_stages.summary: For the 3-dimensional case, the hyperbolic metric is encoded by a PSL(2,C)-invariant Crofton measure on totally geodesic planes, while cubulations are encoded by atomic measures coming from lifts of immersed almost-totally-geodesic surfaces whose limit measures converge weak-* to the Crofton measure.

proof_stages.label: Dimension growth and limiting cubical geometry
proof_stages.summary: The final discussion explains that better approximation requires more intersections among the codimension-one objects, forcing the cubulations to have dimensions tending to infinity and suggesting convergence toward a median closure rather than directly to hyperbolic space.
